PBS Guide
Workflows, tips & daily checklist
🔥 Tap each item when done. Resets automatically at midnight every day.
☀️ Morning Routine
Open Dashboard
Check break-even target, stock alerts, and supplier deadlines. Know your number before you open the shop.
Review Stock Pulse
Filter by Critical and Out-of-Stock. Reorder anything urgent via Reorder Manager in Coach tab.
Check Supplier Deadlines
Any payments due today or overdue? Open Suppliers tab — collapsed cards show balance at a glance. Pay from Supplier Debt bucket first.
Check Expiry Monitor
Stock tab → scroll past Stock Pulse. Items within 30 days are highlighted. Plan returns or promotions before they expire.
🌙 Evening Routine
Review POS Sales
POS tab → Sales History. Check Today filter. Confirm total matches the physical cash + transfer in your drawer.
Log Day in Cash Desk
Cash tab → Log a Day. Enter sales, cash + transfers received (all money in), all expenses. Run 5-Bucket allocation.
Receive Any New Stock
Snap tab → photograph pack → Save & Receive Stock. Or Stock Pulse → Receive button on any product row.
Review 5-Bucket Allocation
Set aside the Supplier Debt portion before any personal spending. This is what separates surviving pharmacies from thriving ones.
📅 Weekly (Fridays)
Export PBS Backup
Guide → Settings → Export Backup. Save the JSON file to Google Drive or WhatsApp it to yourself. One minute. Weekly habit.
Review Week Profit
Cash tab → This Week filter. Is net profit above break-even? Check the summary cards at the top. If not — investigate before Monday.
Open Dashboard every morning and evening. It is your operational command centre.
📊
Today's Snapshot (4 Cards)
Sales, Expenses, This Week, Week Profit. These update automatically when you log a day in Cash Desk. Green = profitable. Red = below target.
🎯
Break-Even Hero
Your daily sales target based on fixed costs. Green when you pass it. Set your actual costs in Guide → Settings → Break-Even Settings. The target auto-recalculates.
📦
Stock Pulse Alerts
Red = Out of Stock (losing money now). Orange = Critical. Yellow = Low. Green = OK. Tap any alert card to go directly to that product.
🏭
Supplier Obligations
Total outstanding across all suppliers with overdue count. Tap the card to open Suppliers tab and action payments.
🧠
AI Morning/Evening Brief
PBS auto-schedules briefings at 5:30 AM and 9:00 PM. Coach tab → ⚡ Generate for AI 5-priority actions. Requires OpenRouter API key (free).
Every product marked Monitor appears in Stock Pulse. No limit on products.
🔴🟠🟡🟢
Status Colours
Green = OK (above minimum). Yellow = Low (at/below min). Orange = Critical (below 50% of min). Red = Out of Stock. Set minimum stock level when adding a product in Snap.
📥
Two Ways to Receive Stock
(1) Snap tab → AI reads pack photo → tap Save & Receive Stock. (2) Stock Pulse → tap Receive on any product row. Both open the Receive Stock modal with the product pre-filled.
📋
Stocktake Mode
Tap the Stocktake button top-right of Stock Pulse. Enter your actual physical counts. PBS calculates variance and adjusts records automatically. Do this monthly.
⏱️
Expiry Monitor
Scroll past Stock Pulse. Colour-coded by time remaining: Red = under 30 days, Orange = 30–60 days, Yellow = 60–90 days. Take action before items expire worthless.
📦
Reorder Manager
Coach tab → scroll down past Priority Actions. Lists all Out-of-Stock and Low products with estimated reorder cost. Tick items, edit quantities, tap Send to Supplier to order via WhatsApp directly.
Snap is your product intelligence engine. The more packs you scan, the richer your database becomes.
🤖
AI Snap (Camera)
Tap AI Snap → photograph any drug pack → Claude AI reads the label and extracts name, strength, category, dosage form, price. Review and confirm. Then choose: Save to Database, Save & Receive Stock, or Sell Now.
🛒
Sell with Snap
After AI reads a pack, tap the purple Sell Now button. PBS sends the product directly to POS basket with name and price pre-filled. Just enter quantity and confirm the sale.
📥
Save & Receive Stock
After AI Snap, tap the green Save & Receive Stock button. Saves product to database then immediately opens the Receive Stock modal with the product name pre-filled — enter supplier, cost, quantity, expiry and save.
🔍
Search Database
Type 2+ characters to search your full product database. Tap any result to view details, edit the product, or open Receive Stock for it directly.
📷
Barcode Scanner
Tap Scan Barcode mode → hold barcode in the box. Auto-detects EAN-13, EAN-8, Code 128, QR codes. Camera uses continuous autofocus — give it 1–2 seconds to lock on.
3-step guided sales flow. Each step auto-advances to the next. The fastest POS for community pharmacy.
1️⃣
Step 1 — Search Product
Type the drug name. Matching products appear as buttons — tap to select. If the product doesn't exist, add it via Snap tab first.
2️⃣
Step 2 — Pack & Quantity
Choose pack size (Tablet / Strip / Bottle etc). Tap the Qty field — number keyboard opens immediately. Type quantity and price auto-fills. Tap Add to Basket.
3️⃣
Step 3 — Payment
Enter customer name (optional — auto-completes from Customers). Enter customer WhatsApp number to send receipt. Apply discount (% or flat ₦). Enter Cash and/or Transfer. Change calculates automatically. Tap Confirm Sale.
🧾
Receipt & Thermal Print
Receipt appears after every sale. Toggle Print ON/OFF at the top of the receipt. Print dialog opens — select your 58mm thermal printer. WhatsApp sends the receipt text to the customer number you entered.
📋
Sales History
Below the basket. Filter: Today / This Week / Month / All Time. Tap Filter for product name search or custom date range. Each sale row is collapsed — tap to expand full items, totals, and reprint receipt.
⏸️
Hold Basket
Tap Hold to park the current basket and start a new sale. Tap Held to resume any parked basket. No limit on held baskets.
Log every day in the evening. Consistency transforms data into financial intelligence.
📝
Log a Day
Enter: Total Sales (from POS Sales History → Today), Cash + Transfer Received (all money that came in today — cash and bank transfers go to the same account so record both), and expenses by category. Tap Save Day Record.
💰
5-Bucket Cash Allocation
Splits today's cash automatically: Inventory 35%, Supplier Debt 25%, Expenses 20%, Owner Draw 15%, Reserve 5%. The discipline of not mixing these buckets is what makes pharmacies financially healthy long-term.
📊
History Summary Cards
Top of Cash History shows 4 coloured total cards for the selected period: Sales, Net Profit, Expenses, Cash In. Instantly see your performance without scrolling through every record.
📅
Date Range Filter
Cash History → tap Filter → set From and To dates. Analyse any specific period. Useful for month-end summaries, tax preparation, or comparing two months.
📤
Export Reports
CSV Export: full date-range data for spreadsheet or accounting software. PDF Daily: one-page formatted summary for the selected period, suitable for printing or sharing.
⚙️ Manage pharmacy identity, voice, API key and app settings here.
🧾 Receipt Options
These settings control what happens automatically after every confirmed sale.
🖨️ Auto-Print Receipt
Opens print dialog after every sale
📱 Auto-Send WhatsApp Receipt
Opens WhatsApp with receipt after every sale
💊 Pharmacy Identity
🧠 AI Model
Choose which AI model to use for Coach, Snap analysis and QB reports. Switch anytime.
💡 Haiku is fastest for daily use. GPT-4o is best for Snap photo reading.
🔑 API Key Setup
💡
Enter your OpenRouter API key to enable AI Coach, AI Snap and QB analysis. Stored only on this device.
Get key: openrouter.ai → Sign in → Keys
⚙️ Break-Even Settings
Enter your monthly fixed costs. Add as many rows as you need.
Calculated Break-Even
— / day
💾 BACKUP & RESTORE
💡
Back up every Friday. Use Save to Drive — on Android it opens your share sheet so you can save directly to Google Drive, WhatsApp yourself the file, or any app. Never clear browser cache without backing up first.
🔑 CHANGE PINs
📱 INSTALL AS APP
🤖
Android (Chrome):
Tap the 3-dot menu (⋮) → "Add to Home Screen" or "Install App" → Install. Opens fullscreen, works offline.
🍎
iPhone (Safari):
Tap the Share button (□↑) → "Add to Home Screen" → Add. Appears as an app icon.
💻
Desktop (Chrome/Edge):
Look for the install icon (⊕) in the address bar → Click Install. Opens in its own window like a native app.
🏠 PBSRx Phoenix RC1
What's new — written for pharmacists and staff
📍 Shelf Location
Every product now has a Shelf Location (e.g., S02, S09). When you search for a product in the POS, the shelf appears below the unit price — so you or your staff can go straight to the right location. You can set or update the shelf in the product edit screen. Shelf is visible in Stock Pulse and Product Search.
⚡ Review Workspace
The Review tab in the navigation bar shows a list of enrichment tasks — things like setting a selling price, linking a supplier, or recording a barcode. Priority 1 tasks must be completed before making your first sale. Tapping any task takes you directly to that product. When you fill in the missing information and save, the task disappears automatically. Nothing is stored — the list is always computed fresh from your product data.
⚠ Set selling prices first — before your first sale.
📊 Product Completeness Score
Every product has a Completeness Score (0–100%) shown in the Review Workspace. A product with just a name scores around 18%. Adding shelf, category, sell unit, and supplier brings it to around 50%. Adding selling price and cost brings it above 70%. The score is computed automatically — it rises as you enrich your data.
🏠 First Inventory Migration (PID MIGRATE)
Step 1: Go to Intake → PID MIGRATE tab.
Step 2: Tap Snapshot & Backup — always do this first.
Step 3: Tap Preview (Dry Run) — this validates your file without importing anything.
Step 4: If the preview shows no errors, tap Run Migration.

Migration takes 5-15 seconds. When it finishes, you will see a Migration Certificate with the session ID. The Review Workspace will then show all enrichment tasks.
📄 PID v1.0 CSV Format
The migration file must have these columns:

Product Name, Owner, Qty, Unit Type, Expiry Date, Unit Cost, Selling Price, Category, Shelf, Notes, Status

Owner must be O (Owner), C (Consignment), or Review.
Status must be Active, Near Expiry, Expired, or No Expiry Recorded.
🚨 Soft Go-Live Workflow (First 14 Days)
Before Day 1: Complete pricing sprint — set selling prices for all daily products. Physically remove expired items from active shelves.

Days 1-7: Record all sales in POS. Receive all stock via Bulk Receive. Check Dashboard every morning. Log any issue in your Phoenix Issue Log.

Days 8-14: Enrich data using Review Workspace. Set isFastMover for products you sell daily. First cycle count of S11 (controlled medicines).
🏃 Review Workspace Workflow
1. Tap the ⚡ Review button in the navigation bar.
2. Filter by Priority 1 first — these are the most important.
3. Tap "Set Price →" on any product.
4. Enter the selling price and cost price in the product form.
5. Save and return — the task is gone.
6. Work through 10-20 tasks per session during quiet periods.
🔧 Administrator Guide
Owner operations — backup, restore, migration, recovery
💾 Backup Procedure
Option A — Export CSV: Go to Stock → View & Edit All → Export. This saves your product catalogue as a CSV file.

Option B — Migration Snapshot: Go to Intake → PID MIGRATE → Snapshot & Backup. This saves a complete database snapshot (products + stock + GRN records) to your browser's local storage.

Recommended frequency: Daily before major operations, and always before any import.
↩ Restore & Rollback
If a migration produces unexpected results:

1. Go to Intake → PID MIGRATE.
2. Scroll to the Emergency Rollback section.
3. Tap "Restore Pre-Migration Snapshot."
4. Confirm when prompted.

The rollback restores PBSRx to the exact state it was in when the snapshot was taken. Only the snapshot from the current session is available — not older sessions.
📥 CSV Import — Product Catalogue
To import a product catalogue from a spreadsheet:

1. Go to Intake → IMPORT CSV tab.
2. Tap "Tap to select CSV or Excel file."
3. Select your file.
4. PBSRx reads the columns automatically.

Required column: Generic Name (or Product Name, Name, Item Name).
Optional columns: Brand, Strength, Form, Category, Cost Price, Sell Price, Qty on Hand, Shelf, Ownership, Notes.

Existing products (matched by name) have their prices updated. New names create new products.
📋 Migration History
Go to Intake → PID MIGRATE → Migration History section. Tap it to load all recorded migration sessions. Each session shows the Session ID, date, products created, stock batches posted, and status. Migration certificates are stored permanently in PBSRx and survive app restarts.
⚠ Recovery Procedures
App shows blank screen: Force-close the browser, clear the browser cache for the app URL only (not all data), reload.

Products missing after reload: Check browser storage — go to browser Settings → Site Data → PBSRx. If storage was cleared externally, re-import from your last CSV backup.

POS not finding a product: Check that the product exists in Stock Pulse. If not, add it via Intake → New Product. If it exists but has zero stock, receive stock via Bulk Receive.

Supplier balance wrong: Go to Intake → Suppliers → view the supplier → check payment history against expected payments. Log a correcting payment if needed.
🔍 Troubleshooting
Q: The Review Workspace shows thousands of tasks.
A: This is expected after migration. Focus on Priority 1 only first (selling prices). Work through the rest during quiet periods over 2-4 weeks.

Q: Stock Pulse shows no products.
A: No products are flagged as Fast Movers yet. Go to Stock → View & Edit All, select your daily-dispensing products, and tick "Monitor in Stock Pulse" (Fast Mover).

Q: Supplier balance is wrong.
A: Migration GRN records have invoiceAmt=0 — they create no liability. Only real Bulk Receive sessions create supplier debt. Check that your opening balance was set correctly in Intake → Suppliers → Edit.

Q: A product sold at ₦0.
A: Selling price was not set before the sale. Open Review Workspace → Priority 1 → find the product → set the selling price. The historical sale record shows ₦0 and cannot be edited, but future sales will be correct.
📞 Phoenix Issue Log (First 14 Days)
During the Soft Go-Live period, log every issue in this format:

• Date: [date]
• Severity: Critical / High / Medium / Low
• Screen: POS / Dashboard / Stock Pulse / etc.
• What happened: [description]
• Screenshot: Volume Down + Power button simultaneously

Batch all Medium and Low issues for the next scheduled Phoenix work package. Critical issues (data loss, POS failure, financial error) should be reported immediately.

📦 Selling Units
Define how this product is sold. Unit 1 is the base unit.
Unit 1 — Base Unit
BASE
= 1
Unit 2
= qty of Unit 1
Unit 3
= qty of Unit 1
Example: tablet (base) → strip (12 tablets, ₦220) → pack (96 tablets, ₦1,500)
